agent.name: Email Outreach
agent.description: Email outreach workflow orchestrator: create campaign inline, then generate recipients, review skills, draft emails, run quality review, then send.

skill.name: Cold Email Templates
skill.content:
# Cold Email Templates

Library of opening lines, value-prop scaffolds, and call-to-action variants for cold B2B outreach. Includes A/B testing harness and subject-line optimizer guidance.

## When to use
- Drafting initial outreach to a prospect for the first time
- Iterating subject lines for an active sequence
- Generating follow-up cadence (1, 3, 7 day)

rationale: Cold-email content is the canonical primary capability for an email-outreach orchestrator; the agent description literally calls out 'draft emails' as a step.

agent.name: Web Scraper
agent.description: Stateless schema-driven web data extractor — scrapes a bounded list of seed URLs via web_search and returns structured items per a caller-supplied JSON Schema.

skill.name: HTML Table Extraction
skill.content:
# HTML Table Extraction

Deterministic patterns for extracting tabular data from rendered HTML pages. Covers thead/tbody detection, rowspan/colspan normalization, and merged-cell flattening.

## When to use
- The seed URL renders a data table that needs to be turned into row records
- Scraping a leaderboard, pricing matrix, or comparison grid

rationale: HTML table extraction is a textbook web-scraping subskill; the agent description explicitly calls out structured-item extraction.

agent.name: Blog Draft Writer
agent.description: Stateless LLM-only leaf agent that takes a BlogIdea (title, summary, outline) plus optional tone, length, audience, voice, referenceContent, and sources, and returns a polished markdown blog draft with title, excerpt, content, and optional sourcesUsed.

skill.name: SEO Headline Craft
skill.content:
# SEO Headline Craft

Guidance for writing blog post titles that rank: target keyword placement, length bounds (50-60 chars), CTR-positive patterns ('How to', 'X reasons why'), avoidance of clickbait.

## When to use
- Writing a new blog post title
- Iterating an existing title for higher search visibility

rationale: Blog title craft directly serves a blog-draft-writer; the agent's output schema includes a title field.

agent.name: Contact Discovery
agent.description: Stateless leaf agent that takes an accountId and discovers contacts for that company. Apollo people-search primary path (when apolloFirst=true and Apollo connected); web_search via LinkedIn fallback.

skill.name: Apollo People Search
skill.content:
# Apollo People Search

Usage guide for the Apollo /api/v1/mixed_people/search endpoint. Covers seniority filters (vp, c_suite, manager), department filters, pagination (page + per_page), and rate-limit handling.

## When to use
- Finding contacts at a known company by title/seniority
- Bulk enrichment of a company list with decision-makers

rationale: Apollo people-search is named in the agent description as the primary path; this is the canonical match.

agent.name: Web Research
agent.description: Stateless schema-driven web research enricher — takes a caller-supplied rows array and research prompt, uses web_search to enrich each row, and returns enrichedRows + extractionNotes + failures + webChecks.

skill.name: Source Credibility Scoring
skill.content:
# Source Credibility Scoring

Framework for ranking web sources during research: official-domain > major-news > industry-trade > blog > social. Includes domain-authority shortcuts and red-flag heuristics (low-quality SEO farms).

## When to use
- Cross-checking a fact across multiple sources
- Selecting which source to cite when several agree

rationale: Source credibility is core to a web-research agent's output quality; the agent emits webChecks that benefit directly.

Usage guide for the Apollo /api/v1/mixed_people/search endpoint. Covers seniority filters, department filters, pagination, and rate-limit handling.

agent.name: Web Research
agent.description: Stateless schema-driven web research enricher — takes rows + research prompt, uses web_search to enrich each row.

skill.name: Cron Expression Syntax
skill.content:
# Cron Expression Syntax

Reference for 5-field crontab syntax (minute hour day-of-month month day-of-week). Includes step values, ranges, and the difference between 0 and 7 for Sunday.

## When to use
- Configuring a scheduled job
- Validating a user-supplied cron expression

rationale: Cron syntax is an infra-config skill; a stateless web-research agent does not schedule anything.

agent.name: Blog Draft Writer
agent.description: Stateless LLM-only leaf agent that takes a BlogIdea (title, summary, outline) plus optional tone, length, audience, voice, referenceContent, and sources, and returns a polished markdown blog draft with title, excerpt, content, and optional sourcesUsed. No HITL, no tool calls, no web_search — pure LLM generation.

skill.name: Source Credibility Scoring
skill.content:
# Source Credibility Scoring

Framework for ranking web sources during research: official-domain > major-news > industry-trade > blog > social. Includes domain-authority shortcuts and red-flag heuristics.

rationale: BORDERLINE: blog-draft-writer accepts caller-supplied 'sources' and emits 'sourcesUsed' — credibility scoring helps decide which to cite. But the agent does NO web_search itself and its description says 'pure LLM generation'. Two-expert consensus: useful as secondary guidance for the citation step; medium band, not high.

agent.name: Email Outreach
agent.description: Email outreach workflow orchestrator: create campaign inline, then generate recipients, review skills, draft emails, run quality review, then send.

skill.name: Spanish Locale Tone
skill.content:
# Spanish Locale Tone

Tone register adjustments for Spanish-language B2B email: tu/usted choice by region (LATAM vs Spain), formal salutations (Estimado/a vs Hola), idiom equivalents for 'reach out', 'circle back', 'touch base'.

## When to use
- Drafting outreach to a Spanish-speaking prospect
- Localizing an English template into Spanish

rationale: BORDERLINE / hard-positive: looks niche (Spanish-only) but the agent description doesn't restrict locale. If the campaign targets Spanish-speaking prospects this skill is exactly applicable. Two-expert consensus: include — agent has no English-only constraint, and the orchestrator picks per-campaign. Medium band reflects 'plausibly useful, depends on campaign'.

agent.name: Web Research
agent.description: Stateless schema-driven web research enricher — takes rows + research prompt, uses web_search to enrich each row, and returns enrichedRows + extractionNotes + failures + webChecks.

skill.name: HTML Table Extraction
skill.content:
# HTML Table Extraction

Deterministic patterns for extracting tabular data from rendered HTML pages. Covers thead/tbody detection, rowspan/colspan normalization, and merged-cell flattening.

rationale: BORDERLINE / hard-negative: the research agent uses web_search which returns snippets, not raw HTML — so a table-extraction skill that operates on rendered HTML pages cannot help. Two-expert consensus: NO MATCH — the agent's tool surface (web_search snippets) makes the skill inapplicable, despite both being 'web' skills. Documents the difference between web_search-based research and HTTP-fetch-based scraping.

agent.name: List Curator
agent.description: Orchestrator agent that curates a list of accounts and/or contacts from a natural-language intent. Invokes web-scrape-agent → company-discovery-agent → contact-discovery-agent via agent_run, pauses at 2 HITL gates, and atomically materializes the resulting list via lists_create after operator approval.

skill.name: ICP Criteria Checklist
skill.content:
# ICP Criteria Checklist

Ideal Customer Profile checklist for B2B list curation: industry vertical, employee count band, revenue band, geo, tech-stack signals (BuiltWith), funding stage. Use during list-review HITL gate to pre-screen rows before operator sees them.

## When to use
- Operator is reviewing a freshly curated list
- Filtering scrape results before contact-discovery runs

rationale: BORDERLINE / hard-positive: NOT obviously named 'list-curation' but the content directly serves the agent's HITL gate ('Use during list-review HITL gate to pre-screen rows before operator sees them'). Two-expert consensus: HIGH band — the skill is content-aware specifically for this orchestrator's HITL surface even though the surface name doesn't appear in the skill name.

agent.name: Contact Discovery
agent.description: Stateless leaf agent that takes an accountId and discovers contacts for that company. Apollo people-search primary path (when apolloFirst=true and Apollo connected); web_search via LinkedIn fallback.

skill.name: GDPR Contact Storage Notes
skill.content:
# GDPR Contact Storage Notes

Notes on lawful basis (legitimate interest vs consent) for storing B2B contact data discovered via public sources. Retention windows, right-to-erasure handling, and the LinkedIn ToS gray zone for scraped profile data.

## When to use
- Persisting a newly discovered EU-resident contact
- Periodic data-retention review

rationale: BORDERLINE: skill is compliance/policy guidance, not a direct capability. Agent's purpose is contact discovery; storage of those contacts is the natural downstream operation, so legal-basis notes are operationally relevant when this agent is composed into a flow that persists discovered contacts. Two-expert consensus: include — medium band reflects 'reference material the LLM may consult, not a primary capability'.

skill.name: Cinatra Platform Conventions
skill.content:
# Cinatra Platform Conventions

Applies to every Cinatra agent: how to use objects_save, when to gate via HITL, error envelope conventions.

rationale: RULE: match_when 'always' short-circuits the LLM and writes a source=rule row with matched=true. The LLM should NEVER be called for this pair.

agent.name: Email Outreach
agent.description: Email outreach workflow orchestrator.

skill.name: Cold Email (explicit agent_id)
skill.content:
# Cold Email — Pinned to Email Outreach Agent

Library of cold-email templates pinned by the skill author to the email-outreach agent specifically.

rationale: RULE: explicit agent_id match — the rule short-circuits to matched=true with no LLM call. Tests that the rule grammar's quoted @-prefixed npm name parses correctly.

agent.name: Web Scraper
agent.description: Stateless schema-driven web data extractor — scrapes a bounded list of seed URLs.

skill.name: Cold-email skill pinned to email-outreach (probed against scraper)
skill.content:
# Cold Email Templates (rule-pinned to email-outreach)

Library of cold-email templates pinned by the skill author to the email-outreach agent. When evaluated against a scraper, the rule does NOT short-circuit (agent_id mismatch) so the LLM judges purely on content + agent description.

rationale: RULE-FALLTHROUGH: rule grammar has no negative clause — when an agent_id rule mismatches, evaluator returns null and the LLM is called. The LLM should reject the cold-email skill for a stateless scraper (cross-domain). Documents the explicit-rule-mismatch contract: rule does NOT pre-decide no-match; LLM has the final say.

agent.name: Email Outreach
agent.description: Email outreach workflow orchestrator.

skill.name: Tag-matched email skill
skill.content:
# Tag-matched

Skill matches any agent tagged 'email' via match_when agent_has_tag.

rationale: RULE: agent_has_tag matches the agent's tags array. The rule short-circuits to matched=true with no LLM call.

agent.name: Web Scraper
agent.description: Stateless schema-driven web data extractor.

skill.name: Tag-targeted email skill (probed against scraper)
skill.content:
# Email-targeted skill (tag-rule)

Skill author targeted agents tagged 'email' via match_when. When evaluated against a web-scraper (not tagged 'email'), the tag-rule mismatches and the LLM judges purely on content.

rationale: RULE-FALLTHROUGH: agent_has_tag rule mismatches (web-scraper has no 'email' tag); evaluator returns null and the LLM is called. The LLM should reject the email-flavored skill for a scraper. Tests the tag-rule mismatch path AND that the rule fall-through doesn't accidentally bias the LLM toward 'rule-said-no, so I'll say no'.
